mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a self-hosted AI platform designed for user-friendly interaction with various LLM runners like Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It offers effortless setup and extensibility, making it suitable for both personal and enterprise use. Key trade-offs include the need for local hosting and management of resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Open WebUI CLI to manage your deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.10/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary and makes it executable.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.10/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the required version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to interact with the new cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
Deploy Open WebUI using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.10 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```

> Add the Open WebUI Helm repository.
> This command installs Open WebUI in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaler configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description:
Be aware of potential costs associated with running the cluster.

```bash

echo 'Monitor your GCP billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

```

> This is a reminder to keep track of your cloud costs.
commands: echo 'Monitor your GCP billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
Remove Open WebUI and its associated resources. This will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

> This command uninstalls the Open WebUI release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup your current deployment, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.10 --namespace open-webui

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

```

> Backup the current deployment.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image version may not exist or is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image version in your Helm chart and ensure it matches the available tags.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may not be starting due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n open-webui' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is created and check the service type and port configurations.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama models, improved API integration features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n open-webui' showing the pods in 'Running' status.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.10/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui
gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1
helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding.
